input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition on the project name convention. In your `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- properties shared by all projects -->

  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this approach:** `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated before NuGet package props, so you can't condition on `IsTestProject` (it isn't set yet). The project-name convention (`.Tests` suffix) is reliable and evaluated immediately.

If your test projects don't follow a consistent naming pattern, the alternative is to place a second `Directory.Build.props` in your test directory — MSBuild only imports the **nearest** one, so the inner file would need to `<Import>` the outer one explicitly:

```xml
<!-- tests/Directory.Build.props -->
<Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..'))" />

<PropertyGroup>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

The name-convention approach is simpler when all test projects share a suffix.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question — no codebase exploration needed.

The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of a test-related package reference or a naming convention. The most robust approach for MSTest + MTP is to condition on whether the project references `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` or `MSTest` packages, or to use a convention like the project name ending in `.Tests`.


The cleanest approach is to condition on the project name using a pattern like `.Tests`, since MSTest's `IsTestProject` property might not be available early enough in the `Directory.Build.props` evaluation phase. Alternatively, checking for the presence of test-related package references works but requires more setup.
